user_query:
I’m preparing a concise, data-backed update for my crew chief. Please do the following using the files in this workspace:

1) Inspect the input/logs directory to discover all run log CSVs (don’t hardcode specific filenames). Implement and run a small command-line script that aggregates these logs into a single session-level report. Save the script as tools/build_session_summary.py and invoke it so that it reads all CSVs under input/logs and writes the report to output/session_summary.csv.

- The report must group by session_date and include exactly these columns: session_date, track, session_label, total_runs, best_et_s, avg_et_s, stdev_et_s, avg_reaction_s, red_light_count, left_lane_runs, right_lane_runs, within_0.02_count.
- Compute metrics as follows:
  - total_runs: number of rows in the session.
  - best_et_s: minimum et_s in the session.
  - avg_et_s: mean of et_s, rounded to 3 decimals.
  - stdev_et_s: sample standard deviation of et_s (ddof=1), rounded to 3 decimals. If only one run exists, output 0.000.
  - avg_reaction_s: mean of reaction_time_s, rounded to 3 decimals.
  - red_light_count: count of rows where red_light is true.
  - left_lane_runs and right_lane_runs: counts by lane value.
  - within_0.02_count: number of runs whose et_s is within ±0.02 seconds of that session’s avg_et_s (use the unrounded mean for this test; count them before rounding the output value).

2) Identify the most recent session by session_date and draft an email to the crew chief summarizing that session. Use input/team_contacts.json to populate recipients:
- To: crew_chief email
- CC: data_analyst email
- From: driver email
- Subject: Session Summary - {session_date} - {track}
- Body: Briefly describe the session_label, date, and track, then list these figures from output/session_summary.csv for that session: total_runs, best_et_s, avg_et_s, stdev_et_s, avg_reaction_s, red_light_count, left_lane_runs, right_lane_runs, within_0.02_count. Close by noting the report path (output/session_summary.csv) and inviting any setup or tuning questions.

Save the email draft as output/latest_session_email.txt. Make sure the numbers in the email body match the corresponding row in output/session_summary.csv for the most recent session. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
